---
rex: []
---

Promote a validated DEVLOG draft from `.claude/sessions/pending-devlog.md` into `.claude/dev-docs/DEVLOG.md`.

## Input

`.claude/sessions/pending-devlog.md` is generated by the Stop hook `draft_devlog.py` when ≥3 real code/infra files were modified during the session AND no DEVLOG entry exists yet for today. Format:

```markdown
---
validated: false
date: YYYY-MM-DD
---

## YYYY-MM-DD — <one-line title?>

### Why
?

### What changed
- `path/to/file` — ?
- ...

### Tests
?

### Reste à faire
?

---
<!-- Auto-captured context (do not edit, used by /devlog-promote)
files_modified: N
commits_since_session_start:
  - sha summary
branch: <branch>
session_window: <ts1> → <ts2>
-->
```

## What to do

1. Read `.claude/sessions/pending-devlog.md`. If absent → print "No DEVLOG draft pending" and stop.

2. Parse the front-matter block (between the first two `---`). Read `validated`.
   - If `validated: false` (or missing) → print "DEVLOG draft is not validated — set `validated: true` after filling all `?` placeholders" and stop.

3. Validate the body has no remaining `?` placeholders in the four sections (Why / What changed / Tests / Reste à faire). The acceptable filler for "no change" is the literal string `n/a`.
   - If any `?` remains in those sections → list which sections still have `?` and stop. Do NOT modify DEVLOG.md.

4. Extract the **promotable block**: everything between the closing `---` of the front-matter and the next horizontal rule `---` that introduces the auto-context comment. The promoted block starts with `## YYYY-MM-DD — <title>` and ends just before the auto-context HTML comment.

5. Open `.claude/dev-docs/DEVLOG.md`. Insert the promotable block:
   - **Anchor**: just after the file's `# DEVLOG\n\n---\n\n` header (most recent entry on top — DEVLOG is reverse-chronological by convention).
   - Add a trailing `\n\n---\n\n` separator after the new entry.
   - Preserve the rest of the file byte-for-byte.

6. Delete `.claude/sessions/pending-devlog.md`.

7. Report: `Promoted DEVLOG entry for YYYY-MM-DD into .claude/dev-docs/DEVLOG.md.`

## Rules

- **Never edit** the user-written sections (Why / What changed / Tests / Reste à faire) — inject verbatim.
- **Never inject** the auto-context comment block — it stays out of DEVLOG (it's only metadata for the promotion step itself).
- **Never overwrite** an existing entry for the same date — if `## <date>` already exists in DEVLOG.md head, stop and report "DEVLOG already has an entry for <date> — manually merge or delete it before re-running".
- **Abort cleanly** on malformed front-matter (missing `validated` key, no closing `---`, etc.) — leave `pending-devlog.md` untouched.

## When to use

- End of session, after `draft_devlog.py` has populated `pending-devlog.md` and you have filled the four sections + flipped `validated: true`.
- Manually, if you hand-wrote a draft following the same format.

## Counter-example

Do NOT run this command if `validated: false` or if any `?` remains — the command will refuse and print which sections need work. Edit the pending file first.
